claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let me help you check your eligibility for Child Benefit. 

First, I need to understand your situation regarding the child or children you're claiming for.

**How old is the child you want to claim Child Benefit for?**

Please tell me:
- Is the child **under 16 years old**?
- Is the child **16 to 19 years old**?
- Or is the child **20 or over**?

If you have multiple children, we can go through them one at a time.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex is 6 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me work through this with you.

So you have **Alex, who is 6 years old** and lives with you at home. Since Alex is under 16, let's continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Since Alex is only 6 years old, they wouldn't be working. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:** Is Alex receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?

At 6 years old, I expect the answer is no, but I need to confirm this to continue the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name. They're only 6, so they don't get anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Since Alex is only 6 years old, they wouldn't be in any apprenticeship program. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status. 

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

This is a key requirement for Child Benefit. If you live in the UK on a regular basis, you should meet this requirement. 

Alternatively, if you don't normally live in the UK, you may still qualify if you meet certain special circumstances such as:
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- Being a Crown servant living abroad
- Other specific situations

So, do you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Since you normally live in the UK (in Manchester), you meet the residency requirement. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to confirm your responsibility for Alex.

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You mentioned that Alex lives with you at home, which would meet this requirement. Being "responsible" means either:
- Living with the child, OR
- Contributing to their upkeep (paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their care)

Since Alex lives with you, you clearly meet this requirement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Important question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example:
- Alex's other parent
- Another relative or guardian
- Anyone else

If someone else is claiming and you both agree that you should claim instead, that can be transferred. But I need to know if there's anyone else currently claiming for Alex.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Since no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check Alex's living situation. You mentioned Alex lives with you at home, but I need to confirm there are no special circumstances.

**Is Alex currently:**
- In hospital?
- In local authority care?
- Being fostered?
- Or living normally at home with you?

Based on what you've told me, it sounds like Alex is living normally at home with you. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Alex lives at home with me normally - not in hospital, not in care, and not fostered. Just a regular home situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
One final question:

**Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, are you receiving any payments from the local council specifically for Alex's accommodation or upkeep? This would be different from other benefits you might receive.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. I'm supporting Alex myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

Based on the information you've provided, **you meet all the eligibility requirements** for claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

### Why you're eligible:
✅ Alex is 6 years old (under 16)  
✅ Alex is not working or receiving benefits in their own right  
✅ You live in the UK (Manchester)  
✅ You're responsible for Alex (living together)  
✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex  
✅ Alex lives at home with you in a normal living situation  
✅ The local council isn't paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance  

### Next Steps - How to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate

### Important Things to Know:

⚠️ **High Income Child Benefit Charge**: If you or your partner earn over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay a tax charge. However, you can still claim Child Benefit and choose not to receive the payments while still getting National Insurance credits.

💷 **Payment Schedule**: Child Benefit is normally paid every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits).

### Ready to Apply?

You can find all the application details here: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or the application process?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), the claimant is responsible for Alex, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Alex lives at home normally (not in hospital, care, or fostered), and the local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility requirements are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for their 6-year-old child Alex who lives with them in Manchester. No disqualifying factors were identified.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), the claimant is responsible for Alex, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Alex lives at home normally (not in hospital, care, or fostered), and the local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility requirements are met.
